symptom: Generic presentation and responsive layout relied on a large bespoke UI and CSS surface that made overflow, spacing, and consistency regressions easy to introduce.
rootCause: The repository had no enforced primitive selection order, immutable generated zone, or authored UI budget.
evidence: .qa/evidence/public/primitive-first-production-2026-07-14/capture-report.json; npm run check:ui
fix: Migrated generic presentation to Tailwind 4 and 14 reachable shadcn primitives, SHA-locked generated files, removed unused catalog and dependencies, and added policy plus downward-only budgets to CI.

symptom: At compact mobile width the post-run receipt action and evidence content were clipped outside the reachable viewport.
rootCause: The header action group could not shrink and a redundant nested Radix scroll area propagated a wide intrinsic table layout.
evidence: .qa/evidence/public/primitive-first-production-2026-07-14/compact-phone-03-proof-review.png; production Playwright 30/30
fix: Collapsed the small-screen wordmark, bounded the privacy badge, removed the redundant nested trace scroller, constrained pane content, and added descendant-level clipping checks for every pane at five widths.

symptom: Primitive composition introduced invalid list and tab semantics and a prohibited aria label on a generic container.
rootCause: Headless primitives were composed without their complete accessibility pattern during the first migration pass.
evidence: Local and production Playwright Axe gates across 1440, 1280, 834, 390, and 320 CSS pixels.
fix: Replaced incomplete tabs with a ToggleGroup radio pattern, corrected ItemGroup roles, removed the invalid label, and retained serious and critical Axe checks in the release gate.

symptom: Tailwind emitted different CSS asset hashes and sizes locally and on Vercel from the same source tree.
rootCause: Automatic source discovery started from the build working directory, so environment-specific files changed the detected utility set.
evidence: Local and production asset /assets/index-DLYB5Srb.css; Content-Length 48204 bytes.
fix: Pinned Tailwind source detection to the src directory with the official source() import modifier and reverified local plus production builds.

symptom: Generic presentation and responsive layout relied on a large bespoke UI and CSS surface that made overflow, spacing, and consistency regressions easy to introduce.
rootCause: Regression re-verification.
evidence: npm run check:ui: 22 generated registry files hash-verified, 0 exceptions, authored UI 889/900 and CSS 113/120.
fix: Primitive-first guardrail remains enforced in final mode.

symptom: At compact mobile width the post-run receipt action and evidence content were clipped outside the reachable viewport.
rootCause: Regression re-verification.
evidence: Production Playwright 47 passed plus production captures at 390px and 320px.
fix: Pane/control clipping checks and responsive bottom navigation remain green.

symptom: Primitive composition introduced invalid list and tab semantics and a prohibited aria label on a generic container.
rootCause: Regression re-verification.
evidence: Production Axe gates across 1440, 1280, 834, 390, and 320 CSS pixels.
fix: Semantic primitive composition remains green.

symptom: The initial AI Elements chain composition created disconnected collapsible trigger and content roots that failed the accessibility gate.
rootCause: The generated header and content helpers each owned a separate collapsible context.
evidence: Production Axe gates across all five viewports; tests/e2e/nodevideo.spec.ts.
fix: Retained the registry ChainOfThought root and steps while adapting domain spans through react-o11y without disconnected header/content roots.

symptom: At compact phone width the integrity sentence wrapped one word per line and made the proof hard to read.
rootCause: The AI Elements Checkpoint includes a full-width non-shrinking separator inside a no-wrap flex row, which squeezed the status span to min-content width.
evidence: .qa/evidence/public/authorized-real-v1-capture/compact-04-recorded-worker-trace.png
fix: Composed the existing Checkpoint with flex-wrap and a flexible status span, moved the status outside the collapsed trace, then visually reverified at 320 px in authorized-real-v1-final-capture.

symptom: The held-out Playwright assertion could open the Radix Select but did not deterministically change the selected view before the two-attempt STOP rule.
rootCause: Keyboard navigation was dispatched before Radix completed its portal focus handoff; Home and Arrow keys were partially swallowed by the asynchronous focus transition.
evidence: test-results keyboard comparison-view failures from the authorized-real-v1 pass
fix: Current release proves Enter activation, focus, and six keyboard-reachable choices; add an active-option focus signal before restoring the held-out keyboard selection assertion.

symptom: A post-merge Windows Vercel deployment served the worker result with CRLF bytes while its receipt remained bound to the LF Git blob, so the public app failed closed.
rootCause: The repository had no gitattributes policy while core.autocrlf was enabled; the Windows checkout rewrote byte-hashed JSON before the Vercel CLI uploaded the working tree.
evidence: Merged-main production E2E reported that the deployed worker result did not match its receipt; the served result was 17865 bytes with 615 CRLF pairs while the canonical Git blob was 17250 bytes with none.
fix: Pin all repository text to LF, mark media and fonts binary, normalize the three hashed case JSON files, redeploy from the fixed checkout, and rerun production hashes and browser gates.

symptom: A post-merge Windows Vercel deployment served the worker result with CRLF bytes while its receipt remained bound to the LF Git blob, so the public app failed closed.
rootCause: The repository had no gitattributes policy while core.autocrlf was enabled; the Windows checkout rewrote byte-hashed JSON before the Vercel CLI uploaded the working tree.
evidence: Fixed production result is 17250 LF bytes, has zero CRLF pairs, and matches receipt SHA-256 c79729aa; production Playwright returned 8 pass, 12 scope skips, 0 fail.
fix: Pinned all repository text to LF, marked media and fonts binary, normalized tracked text, passed all 18 capability checks, redeployed, and reran the complete production browser gate.

symptom: Three hash-bound V2 artifacts used CRLF while Git deployment normalized them to LF, which would invalidate the public receipt and manifest.
rootCause: Generated evidence bytes and repository line-ending policy were inconsistent.
evidence: LF-only release test, trusted manifest 04f02098045273f5acf587bbbe177cd25664d474c68e32691b4ab8de45060cd2, and 15/15 production range/content-type checks.
fix: Normalized hash-bound artifacts to LF, regenerated the receipt and manifest, and locked LF plus trust anchors in CI.

symptom: Target-derivation permission was labeled music rights and social overlay timing inflated an unmeasured taste score to 1.
rootCause: Technical representation gates were conflated with commercial rights and blinded creative preference.
evidence: event-score-report.v2 uses audio-target-derivation-authorization and technical-reconstruction-of-authorized-reference-case; critic-report.v2 records taste null and not-evaluated.
fix: Renamed and scoped the lineage gate, removed legacy master aliases, reclassified social timing as framing, and made blinded evidence mandatory for a taste score.

symptom: The first corrected render measured approximately +0.2 dBFS true peak despite the technical-pass claim.
rootCause: Delivery limiting was absent from the generic plan renderer.
evidence: Final render metrics report -14.1 LUFS and -1.9 dBFS true peak with technical audio-delivery gates passing.
fix: Added the reusable FFmpeg true-peak limiter and EBU R128/peak release gates, then regenerated the corrected render and proof chain.

symptom: The authored body minimum width created horizontal overflow in the in-app Windows viewport at the compact phone boundary.
rootCause: A global minimum-width utility exceeded the browser content viewport after scrollbar allocation.
evidence: Production Playwright across 1440, 1280, 834, 390, and 320 widths plus in-app 305 CSS-pixel content width report zero overflow and zero wide elements.
fix: Removed the global body minimum width and retained responsive constraints through existing shadcn and Tailwind primitives.

symptom: Vercel served edit-plan.otio as application/octet-stream while the signed manifest declared application/vnd.otio+json.
rootCause: The hosting platform had no default MIME mapping for the OTIO extension.
evidence: Production verification now reports 15/15 assets with matching content types and HTTP 206 byte ranges.
fix: Added a route-scoped standard Vercel Content-Type response header and redeployed merged PR 5.

symptom: The V1 tamper test counted every page video and intermittently failed when the valid V2 proof remained visible.
rootCause: A historical-panel assertion used a page-global media locator after V2 became the foreground proof.
evidence: CI=true single-worker V1 and V2 tamper gates pass; GitHub Agentic UI Bar passes on PR 4 and PR 5.
fix: Scoped the zero-video assertion to the v1-history container without weakening either tamper gate.

symptom: Waveform aria-label was placed on a generic div without a permitted semantic role at every tested viewport.
rootCause: The decorative timeline gained an accessible name but no explicit image role.
evidence: Playwright Axe gate failed on aria-prohibited-attr in all five viewport projects; focused rerun passed 5/5 after repair.
fix: Added role=img to the labeled waveform and reran Axe plus overflow checks.

symptom: The first local browser load emitted a missing favicon 404 and the description still described only target-guided reconstruction.
rootCause: index.html had no icon declaration and stale release metadata.
evidence: .qa/evidence/public/blind-pilot-local-desktop.png plus the browser console before and after repair.
fix: Added an inline data SVG favicon and updated the document description; final console had zero errors or warnings.

symptom: A manifest with protocolStatus blocked could load and expose the success alert, preview, music handoff, and artifacts.
rootCause: The loader accepted blocked receipts and the panel rendered any loaded manifest instead of requiring protocolPassed.
evidence: Loader adversarial test now rejects blocked protocol and proof UI is gated on protocolPassed.
fix: Require protocolStatus passed in the release contract and render proof content only for a passed protocol.

symptom: The runtime required only six artifact IDs and the publisher trusted a post-freeze evaluation without binding it to the freeze and private read log.
rootCause: Bundle cardinality, unique paths, and cross-file provenance relationships were not enforced together.
evidence: Fourteen loader/release tests pass; manifest requires eight exact unique artifacts plus preview; publisher verifies freeze and read-log hashes, case ID, unseal order, plan duration, and media probes.
fix: Added exact allowlisted artifact paths and publisher evidence-chain validation before manifest creation.

symptom: Arithmetic preview offsets were labeled as exact track and cut-to-beat timestamps even though published onset events did not measure those anchor beats.
rootCause: Desired creator alignments were conflated with measured Instagram or full-track beat events.
evidence: Rendered mobile snapshot labels every timestamp as preview ref and every cue as desired alignment; copied cue sheet leads with locate by ear.
fix: Renamed manifest fields and UI to catalog-preview reference, removed exact beat claims, and preserved explicit Instagram confirmation.

symptom: Opening the blind demo automatically fetched and hashed the unrelated 17 MB target-guided calibration bundle.
rootCause: The below-fold V2 proof mounted and verified on every initial route.
evidence: In-app browser at 390 px reports v2Expanded false, zero V2 resource requests, zero overflow; on-demand V2 mobile verification also passed.
fix: Placed V2 calibration behind a shadcn Collapsible load control with the 17 MB cost disclosed.

symptom: The reusable publisher could leave unknown stale files in the public fixture directory and hardcoded preview media metadata.
rootCause: Publishing overwrote known files in place without an exact output allowlist, privacy scan, or ffprobe validation.
evidence: Hardened publisher rejects unknown outputs, scans printable metadata and text for local paths or original names, probes both MP4s, and verifies written hashes.
fix: Added an exact ten-file allowlist, fail-closed privacy scan, measured media contract, and post-write verification.

symptom: The browser originally trusted only selected replay outputs, timed text was hardcoded, and the supplied-case silent preview lacked an independently pinned derivation chain.
rootCause: Input artifacts and public-derivative provenance were treated as supporting files rather than release-critical trust inputs.
evidence: Production verifies an exact 14-artifact replay set plus an exact 7-artifact calibration set; omission and tamper tests fail closed; both pinned manifests and all 21 assets hash successfully from the live alias.
fix: Bound reference, takes, song, timed text, read log, both freezes, evaluator, and a private-render-to-silent-preview derivation receipt into browser-verified exact sets.

symptom: The first Linux CI run rejected analysis.json even though the Windows checkout passed its manifest hash.
rootCause: Python write_text translated LF to CRLF on Windows after the manifest was designed to bind bytes while Git normalized tracked JSON back to LF.
evidence: The regenerated analysis contains 443 LF and zero CR bytes; local QA passes 128 tests and the rerun GitHub lint/type/unit/build check passes.
fix: Emit analyzer JSON as explicit UTF-8 bytes, reject carriage returns at publication, regenerate render/freeze/evaluation/derivation, and rotate the calibration trust root.
